title: physocarpus gracilis
intro: Physocarpus gracilis, also known as Ninebark, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its attractive white or pink flowers and papery, exfoliating bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Physocarpus gracilis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a carrier oil.
